auth_note: Login endpoint: POST /auth/login. Response field: data.accessToken. All test users use password 'NexaDev2026!'

endpoints.description: Authenticate user and return JWT (prerequisite for all tests)

test_cases.name: Create task - minimal fields (title only) as STAFF

db_verification.query: SELECT id, title, status, priority, company_id, created_by_id, deleted_at FROM tasks WHERE title = 'Test task from API plan'
db_verification.expected: Row exists with status=OPEN, priority=NORMAL, companyId matches, deleted_at IS NULL

test_cases.name: Create task - all fields as ADMIN

body.title: Full task with all fields
body.description: A comprehensive task description for testing.

db_verification.query: SELECT t.id, t.priority, COUNT(ta.id) as assignee_count FROM tasks t LEFT JOIN task_assignees ta ON ta.task_id = t.id WHERE t.title = 'Full task with all fields' GROUP BY t.id

test_cases.name: Create task - with entity link as MANAGER

db_verification.query: SELECT entity_type, entity_id FROM tasks WHERE title = 'Task linked to an entity'

test_cases.name: Create task - entityType without entityId (400 - refinement)
Create task - entityId without entityType (400 - refinement)
Create task - invalid entityType (400 or 404 from entity registry)
Create task - invalid assigneeId UUID format (400)
Create task - nonexistent assigneeId (404 from service)
List tasks - no filters as VIEWER

expected.body_check: all returned tasks have staff user as assignee

test_cases.name: List tasks - search by title keyword
List tasks - pagination with limit and offset
List tasks - search returns empty list

endpoints.description: List current user's assigned tasks (STAFF+ required)

test_cases.name: Get my tasks - as ADMIN (assigned to admin)

expected.body_check: total >= 2 (admin is assigned to seed tasks 101, 103)

test_cases.name: Get my tasks - as STAFF (assigned to staff)

expected.body_check: returns only tasks where staff user is an assignee

test_cases.name: Get my tasks - filter by status=OPEN

endpoints.description: Get task detail by ID (VIEWER+ required)

test_cases.name: Get task detail - existing OPEN task as VIEWER
Get task detail - existing COMPLETED task
Get task detail - invalid UUID format (400)
Update task - change title as STAFF

db_verification.query: SELECT title FROM tasks WHERE id = '00000000-0000-4000-a000-000000000101'

test_cases.name: Update task - change priority and description as MANAGER

db_verification.query: SELECT priority, description FROM tasks WHERE id = '00000000-0000-4000-a000-000000000102'

test_cases.name: Update task - clear dueDate (set to null)
Update task - COMPLETED task rejected (422 - BR-TASK-006)
Update task - empty body (400 - refinement: at least one field)

db_verification.query: SELECT status, completed_at FROM tasks WHERE id = '00000000-0000-4000-a000-000000000103'

test_cases.name: Status transition - IN_PROGRESS to COMPLETED (sets completedAt)

db_verification.query: SELECT status, completed_at FROM tasks WHERE id = '00000000-0000-4000-a000-000000000103'

test_cases.note: Run AFTER 'OPEN to IN_PROGRESS' test. Depends on task 103 being IN_PROGRESS.

test_cases.name: Status transition - OPEN to CANCELLED
test_cases.note: Use a newly created task to avoid corrupting seed data. Or use task 101 if tests are ordered.

test_cases.name: Status transition - COMPLETED to IN_PROGRESS rejected (422 - terminal state BR-TASK-006)
Status transition - COMPLETED to OPEN rejected (422 - terminal state)
Status transition - CANCELLED to OPEN rejected (422 - terminal state)
test_cases.note: Depends on created_task_1 being CANCELLED from prior test

test_cases.name: Add assignee - add viewer user to task as STAFF
Add assignee - duplicate assignee (409)
test_cases.note: Admin user (0002) is already assigned to task 101 in seed data

test_cases.name: Remove assignee - remove viewer user from task 101
test_cases.note: Run AFTER 'Add assignee - add viewer user' test to have data to remove

endpoints.description: Soft delete task (STAFF+ for own tasks, MANAGER+ for others)

test_cases.name: Delete task - creator deletes own task as STAFF
test_cases.note: Use the task created by STAFF in the create tests (created_task_1 or a fresh creation). STAFF user is the creator.

db_verification.query: SELECT deleted_at FROM tasks WHERE id = '{{created_task_from_staff}}'

test_cases.name: Delete task - MANAGER deletes another user's task
test_cases.note: Use created_task_3 (created by MANAGER) or a task created by admin.

db_verification.query: SELECT deleted_at FROM tasks WHERE id = '{{created_task_3}}'

test_cases.name: Delete task - ADMIN deletes any task
Delete task - STAFF cannot delete another user's task (403)
test_cases.note: Task 101 was created by admin (createdById=0002), not staff (0010)

test_cases.name: Delete task - already deleted task not visible in list
test_cases.note: After deleting created_task_2, verify it does not appear in GET /tasks

expected.body_check: Soft-deleted task does not appear in list results

test_execution_order: 2. POST /tasks — create tests (happy paths first, save IDs for later use)
10. DELETE /tasks/:id — delete tests (last, as they soft-delete data)

rbac_coverage.SUPER_ADMIN: Full access verified on create, list, my tasks, status, delete
rbac_coverage.MANAGER: Verified on create, update, status transition, delete others' tasks
rbac_coverage.STAFF: Verified on all mutating endpoints; cannot delete non-owned tasks (403)
rbac_coverage.VIEWER: Read-only: can list and get detail; denied on create/update/delete/status/assignees (403)

business_rules_covered: BR-TASK-002: companyId scoping (implicit in all queries)
BR-TASK-003: entityType must be valid + entity must exist if provided
BR-TASK-004: Assignee must exist in same company
BR-TASK-005: task.assigned event emitted (verified via service tests, not HTTP)
BR-TASK-006: Terminal states (COMPLETED/CANCELLED) reject further changes (422)
BR-TASK-008: completedAt auto-set on COMPLETED transition
